message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH = process ALL 346 baskets. FAITHFULNESS = context NLI + STRICT numbers.
BINDING INVARIANTS (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI +
numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; external content re-enters ONLY via the fold-in seam;
computed numbers render ONLY via the verified [#calc:] lane (never [CITE:ev_xxx]); exploratory
execute_python is BARRED from rendering; weight-and-consolidate, never filter.
FENCE: work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree or the
operator's /home/polaris/polaris_project monitoring dir. Report REAL output only; never fake a pass.


YOU ARE THE BUILDER (Opus hands) for "compose". Fable already found the problems and
told you EXACTLY what to change. DO NOT re-investigate, DO NOT hunt for an entrypoint, DO NOT wander.
Apply ONLY these fixes to src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py, in order:
[
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py:3325 (basket loop) and :2221-2222 (sentence loop)",
    "problem": "Even off-loop (after fix #1), _compose_section_per_basket is a strictly serial basket-after-basket, sentence-after-sentence loop — one blocking ~8.9s judge POST at a time — so intra-section verify concurrency is 1 and the raised judge cap (fix #2) is only exploitable across sections/shards. (Dedups 3 fan-out findings.)",
    "fix": "Fan out over baskets with a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor using the battle-tested contextvars-copy pattern from provenance_generator.py:3678-3718 (_parent_ctx.copy().run), workers ~= PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY; verdicts are order-independent, reassemble by index. Optionally also parallelize the per-sentence verify inside _compose_one_basket the same way. Do NOT change verify_fn/redraft semantics — the faithfulness gate stays byte-identical per sentence.",
    "acceptance": "During one section's phase-2, >1 judge POST concurrently in flight (overlapping POST start/end timestamps); section compose output on a fixture is identical (same kept sentences, same [CITE]/[#calc] tokens) to the serial path; per-run judge telemetry (FX-09 ContextVar) still reconciles."
  }
]

For each: make the concrete change at the file:line; then ACTUALLY EXERCISE the touched path (run it,
py_compile is NOT enough — the acceptance observable tells you what to run); then git commit in
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix with an honest message (say PARTIAL if partial). Append one dated line to
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. Report what landed vs what Fable asked. A skipped fix reported is fine; hidden is not.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
3270	    agree_map: Any = None,
3271	    redraft_fn: Optional[Callable[..., str]] = None,
3272	    numeric_key_by_cluster: Optional[dict] = None,
3273	    research_question: str = "",
3274	) -> list[str]:
3275	    """PRIMARY per-section prose producer: compose EVERY basket of the section (the contract
3276	    entities are a SUBSET — this is what moves the scored breadth off the contract-slot bound).
3277	    Returns one composed string per basket, in order. Order-stable.
3278	
3279	    I-deepfix-001 M6 (cross-source analytical synthesis): when ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS`` is ON
3280	    (DEFAULT-OFF => byte-identical; ``edges``/``equiv_clusters``/``agree_map`` are then unused and never
3281	    read), an ADDITIVE pass appends analytical sentences — each ``[verified clause A][engine-LICENSED
3282	    connective][verified clause B]`` spanning TWO baskets — AFTER the per-basket units are built. The
3283	    two atoms keep their own ``[#ev]`` tokens and re-pass the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` per clause; the
3284	    connective carries no token and is licensed by ``cross_source_synthesis.license_relation`` from the
3285	    certified relation engines (``edges`` = ContradictionEdge list, the agreement map). KEEP-ALL: the
3286	    analytical unit is additive on top of the single-source units; the idx8 footprint dedup below never
3287	    collapses it (its two-token footprint is a SUPERSET of either atom's), so no source/basket vanishes.
3288	
3289	    I-beatboth-011 (#1289):
3290	      §3.5 placeholder-leak — DROP any per-basket result that is the internal insufficient-evidence
3291	        marker before appending (the same filter the sibling multi-cite path already applies at the
3292	        `_per_basket_verified_clause` :337-338 precedent). An empty post-suppression section routes
3293	        through the existing gap/abort handling (the producer no longer guarantees one unit per basket
3294	        when that unit would be the internal marker — leaking the marker into report.md is the bug).
3295	      idx8 seen-span — DROP a composed unit ONLY when it is a TRUE duplicate: ALL of its RESOLVED
3296	        (ev_id,start,end) provenance tuples already appear in an emitted sibling in THIS section AND its
3297	        whitespace-normalized text is byte-identical to that sibling (faithfulness-neutral — a true
3298	        duplicate adds nothing). A SUBSET span alone does NOT prove the same claim (Codex #1289 iter-1
3299	        P1: different prose can cite an overlapping span), so a DIFFERING claim that merely shares a
3300	        span subset is KEPT. A unit with NO provenance token is NEVER dropped (zero tokens must not be
3301	        vacuously "all duplicate").
3302	
3303	    I-beatboth-011 keystone-F1 (#1284): when ``PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED`` is ON (DEFAULT-OFF =>
3304	    byte-identical), a basket carrying >=2 corroborating isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members is composed via
3305	    ``compose_basket_multicited_sentence`` — ONE multi-cited sentence surfacing ALL its corroborators
3306	    (the §-1.3 consolidate-keep-all reading) instead of collapsing to the single strongest member.
3307	    A single-``SUPPORTS`` (or zero) basket routes the UNCHANGED ``_compose_one_basket`` path. The
3308	    relational-quantifier guard inside the multi-cited producer drops any unlicensed aggregate predicate;
3309	    each clause still passes the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` per-clause. The downstream §3.5 marker filter
3310	    + idx8 seen-span dedup are applied identically to the multi-cited unit."""
3311	    out: list[str] = []
3312	    seen_spans: set[tuple[str, int, int]] = set()
3313	    seen_texts: set[str] = set()
3314	    # Fix 3b (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop iter 2): citation-insensitive normalized-text -> index in
3315	    # ``out`` of the SURVIVING unit, so a later text-identical duplicate's citations are UNION-consolidated
3316	    # onto the survivor (§-1.3 keep-all) instead of dropped-and-lost.
3317	    text_to_out_idx: dict[str, int] = {}
3318	    # I-arch-011 #1269 B11 (compose-repetition): a SECOND, stronger key — the exact resolved-span
3319	    # FOOTPRINT (frozenset) already emitted -> the union of its kept number tokens. A later unit with
3320	    # the IDENTICAL footprint that adds NO new number is a pure reword of an already-rendered span ->
3321	    # collapse (the 18x-same-span degenerate-repetition defect). This is footprint EQUALITY, not the
3322	    # subset key Codex #1289 rejected, and it is faithfulness-neutral (same span, already verified).
3323	    seen_numbers_by_footprint: dict[frozenset, frozenset[str]] = {}
3324	    _multicited_on = _multicited_compose_enabled()
3325	    for basket in (section_baskets or []):
3326	        # keystone-F1: surface a >=2-corroborator basket as ONE multi-cited sentence (flag-gated,
3327	        # default-OFF). A single-source basket falls through to the UNCHANGED single-basket producer
3328	        # (the multi-cited producer itself returns the same K-span draft for <2 SUPPORTS, but routing
3329	        # explicitly keeps the default-OFF path byte-identical with NO new import/call when the flag is
3330	        # off, and only invokes the new producer for genuinely-corroborated baskets when on).
3331	        if _multicited_on and len(_distinct_origin_supports(basket)) >= 2:
3332	            # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): the corroborated (>=2 distinct-origin SUPPORTS) baskets are
3333	            # the §-1.3 consolidate-keep-all CORE body. When PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY is ON *and* a group-capable
3334	            # redraft_fn is threaded, compose them THROUGH the synth-primary group writer (the stricter
3335	            # per-sentence writer verify wrapper + bounded repair) instead of the verbatim-K-span
3336	            # co-location — while STILL surfacing every distinct-origin corroborator the authored prose did
3337	            # not itself cite (all-corroborator multi-citation preserved; no verify gate relaxed). Flag OFF
3338	            # OR no redraft_fn => the UNCHANGED multi-cited co-location => byte-identical to pre-Wave-3a.
3339	            if redraft_fn is not None and _synth_primary_enabled():
3340	                composed = compose_basket_multicited_synth_primary(
3341	                    basket, evidence_pool, writer_fn=writer_fn, verify_fn=verify_fn,
3342	                    redraft_fn=redraft_fn,
3343	                ) or ""
3344	            else:
3345	                composed = compose_basket_multicited_sentence(
3346	                    basket, evidence_pool, writer_fn=writer_fn, verify_fn=verify_fn,
3347	                ) or ""
3348	        else:
3349	            composed = _compose_one_basket(
3350	                basket, evidence_pool, writer_fn=writer_fn, verify_fn=verify_fn,
3351	                # I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): thread the group-capable re-draft writer so the
3352	                # SYNTH_PRIMARY bounded-repair path can re-call the writer. Default None => byte-identical
3353	                # (the legacy _compose_one_basket path). Wave-3a (#1344): the multi-cited producer above
3354	                # ALSO honours SYNTH_PRIMARY now (compose_basket_multicited_synth_primary), routing
3355	                # corroborated baskets through the same group writer + strict verify while surfacing every
3356	                # corroborator; both single-basket and multi-cite paths share the SYNTH_PRIMARY writer.
3357	                redraft_fn=redraft_fn,
3358	                # I-deepfix-003 (#1374) STEP 5: thread the research_question so the K-span FALLBACK
3359	                # (build_verified_span_draft_multi) applies the off-topic span screen. Default "" =>
3360	                # byte-identical.
3361	                research_question=research_question,
3362	            )
3363	        # §3.5: suppress the internal insufficient-evidence marker before it can leak into report.md.
3364	        # Also skip an empty multi-cited result (a basket with no resolvable span at all) — the §3.5
3365	        # filter for ``_compose_one_basket`` never sees an empty unit (it always returns at least the
3366	        # disclosure), but the multi-cited producer can return ``""`` when even the K-span is absent;
3367	        # an empty unit must not be appended (it would render a blank line, never a silent success).
3368	        if not composed.strip() or composed.strip().startswith("[insufficient verified evidence"):
3369	            continue
3370	        # idx8 (Codex #1289 P1): drop a unit ONLY when it is a true duplicate — its resolved spans are
3371	        # already emitted AND its normalized text is byte-identical to a sibling. Requiring text
3372	        # identity (not merely a span subset) keeps every differing claim. Apply AFTER the §3.5 marker
3373	        # filter so a token-less marker never reaches this check.
3374	        spans = _resolved_spans(composed)
3375	        norm = _dedup_norm(composed)  # Fix 3a: citation-INSENSITIVE text key
3376	        footprint = frozenset(spans)
3377	        # I-arch-011 #1269 B11: footprint-EQUALITY same-span collapse (the 18x-degenerate-repetition
3378	        # defect). A unit whose EXACT footprint was already emitted AND that adds NO new number token is
3379	        # a pure reword of an already-rendered span -> drop (faithfulness-neutral; same verified span).
3380	        # The conservative number carve-out keeps a same-span unit that states a genuinely NEW statistic.
3381	        if footprint and footprint in seen_numbers_by_footprint:
3382	            unit_numbers = _number_tokens(composed)
3383	            if not (unit_numbers - seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint]):
3384	                continue
3385	            seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] = seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] | unit_numbers
3386	        # Fix 3b (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop iter 2): text-identity collapse regardless of which
3387	        # member's span backs it (the ``spans <= seen_spans`` gate is DROPPED). Identical post-strip prose
3388	        # is a duplicate; UNION the duplicate's citations onto the SURVIVING unit (§-1.3 consolidate-keep-
3389	        # all — never drop a corroborating source), then skip the duplicate. Citation-rotated exact
3390	        # duplicates (same prose, different [N]) now collapse where they leaked before.
3391	        if norm and norm in seen_texts:
3392	            surv_idx = text_to_out_idx.get(norm)
3393	            if surv_idx is not None:
3394	                dup_tokens = [f"[#ev:{_e}:{_s}-{_en}]" for (_e, _s, _en) in spans]
3395	                out[surv_idx] = _attach_citation_tokens(out[surv_idx], dup_tokens)
3396	            seen_spans |= spans
3397	            continue
3398	        if footprint and footprint not in seen_numbers_by_footprint:
3399	            seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] = _number_tokens(composed)
3400	        seen_spans |= spans
3401	        seen_texts.add(norm)
3402	        text_to_out_idx[norm] = len(out)
3403	        out.append(composed)
3404	
3405	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 PART 1 (#1344): COMPANION-FIGURE COMPOSE. DEFAULT-ON (OFF => this block is
3406	        # skipped => byte-identical). Surface the same-kind companion percent(s) this basket's members
3407	        # carry but the headline OMITTED — each a VERBATIM span slice re-verified by the UNCHANGED
3408	        # strict_verify (verify_fn) + own-region gate. Route each surfaced unit through the SAME in-scope
3409	        # dedup as `composed` (seen_spans / seen_texts / seen_numbers_by_footprint) so a TRUE duplicate

3480	
3481	        # I-deepfix-001 #1344 (Item 11): L2 ADDITIVE DISTINCT-FACT surfacing. DEFAULT-OFF (OFF => this
3482	        # block is skipped => byte-identical). This is the ADDITIVE arm of sub-topic decomposition: L2's
3483	        # extra distinct facts used to render ONLY as the per-basket fallback when the paraphrase FAILED
3484	        # verify; now, on the SUCCESS path, surface this basket members' OTHER verbatim sentence-units
3485	        # that state a substantive NUMBER the abstractive headline DROPPED (absolute counts, currency,
3486	        # dates, multipliers — the bare integers the writer's numeric-completeness gate does NOT force
3487	        # into the paraphrase). Each is a VERBATIM span slice re-verified by the UNCHANGED strict_verify
3488	        # (verify_fn) + own-region gate, ADDITIVE to (never replacing) `composed`. Route each surfaced
3489	        # unit through the SAME in-scope dedup as `composed` (seen_spans / seen_texts /
3490	        # seen_numbers_by_footprint) so a TRUE duplicate collapses (incl. a unit already surfaced by the
3491	        # companion / qualifier pass above — identical span + text) but a genuinely-new fact is kept.
3492	        # Each kept unit is already [#ev:]-tokened, so it rides the same downstream
3493	        # _rewrite_draft_with_spans + strict_verify tail as every other composed unit.
3494	        if _subtopic_additive_facts_enabled():
3495	            for extra in compose_distinct_fact_units(
3496	                basket, evidence_pool, composed, verify_fn=verify_fn,
3497	            ):
3498	                if not extra or not extra.strip():
3499	                    continue
3500	                x_spans = _resolved_spans(extra)
3501	                x_norm = _dedup_norm(extra)  # Fix 3a: citation-INSENSITIVE key
3502	                x_footprint = frozenset(x_spans)
3503	                if x_footprint and x_footprint in seen_numbers_by_footprint:
3504	                    x_numbers = _number_tokens(extra)
3505	                    if not (x_numbers - seen_numbers_by_footprint[x_footprint]):
3506	                        continue
3507	                    seen_numbers_by_footprint[x_footprint] = seen_numbers_by_footprint[x_footprint] | x_numbers
3508	                # Fix 3b: text-identity collapse regardless of span backing; union citations onto survivor.
3509	                if x_norm and x_norm in seen_texts:
3510	                    _si = text_to_out_idx.get(x_norm)
3511	                    if _si is not None:
3512	                        out[_si] = _attach_citation_tokens(
3513	                            out[_si], [f"[#ev:{_e}:{_s}-{_en}]" for (_e, _s, _en) in x_spans]
3514	                        )
3515	                    seen_spans |= x_spans
3516	                    continue
3517	                if x_footprint and x_footprint not in seen_numbers_by_footprint:
3518	                    seen_numbers_by_footprint[x_footprint] = _number_tokens(extra)
3519	                seen_spans |= x_spans
3520	                seen_texts.add(x_norm)
3521	                text_to_out_idx[x_norm] = len(out)
3522	                out.append(extra)
3523

content.content:
3660	    # decision as before, just dispatched either serially (default) or across a
3661	    # BOUNDED thread pool. Verdicts are independent; results are reassembled in the
3662	    # ORIGINAL order so kept/dropped are byte-identical to the serial path.
3663	    _reanchor_enabled = _provenance_reanchor_enabled()
3664	    _verify_workers = _parallel_verify_workers()
3665	    if _verify_workers < 2 or len(_findings_to_verify) < 2:
3666	        # Serial path (default / single-item): byte-identical to the legacy loop.
3667	        _findings_results = [
3668	            _verify_one_findings_sentence(
3669	                s, evidence_pool,
3670	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3671	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3672	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3673	            )
3674	            for s in _findings_to_verify
3675	        ]
3676	    else:
3677	        # Parallel path: a BOUNDED ThreadPoolExecutor caps concurrency at exactly
3678	        # _verify_workers. A fresh worker thread starts with an EMPTY contextvars
3679	        # context, which would silently drop the per-run judge telemetry (FX-09
3680	        # ContextVar holding the run's {calls, judge_error} dict), the ambient role,
3681	        # and the gate's provider pin. So we capture the PARENT context ONCE here in
3682	        # the calling thread and run each task inside a copy of it — the ContextVar
3683	        # still references the SAME run-telemetry dict object, so the in-place
3684	        # ``_run_tel[...] += 1`` ticks land in the right counter. ``map`` preserves
3685	        # input order => the reassembled list is index-aligned with
3686	        # _findings_to_verify (deterministic kept/dropped, never race-ordered). A
3687	        # worker exception PROPAGATES out of map() (fail-loud — a hard gate /
3688	        # programming defect is never swallowed), exactly as the serial loop raises.
3689	        import concurrent.futures as _futures  # noqa: PLC0415 (lazy: zero OFF-path cost)
3690	        import contextvars as _ctxvars  # noqa: PLC0415
3691	
3692	        # I-arch-011 (Codex FIX-C P1): each worker runs in a COPIED context, so the entailment
3693	        # judge's ``_orc._add_run_cost`` / ``check_run_budget`` mutate the COPY's ``_RUN_COST_CTX``
3694	        # (lost to the parent) — the parallel verify spend would bypass PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN and the
3695	        # run-budget gate. The cost LEDGER stays accurate (``append_cost_ledger_row`` bumps a
3696	        # process-global, lock-protected per-session accumulator, NOT a contextvar). Mirror the
3697	        # credibility_pass offload reconciliation (openrouter_client.ledger_cumulative docstring):
3698	        # snapshot the per-session ledger cumulative before/after the pool and re-add the delta to the
3699	        # PARENT ``_RUN_COST_CTX``, then re-check the budget so the gate is inclusive of the parallel
3700	        # judge spend. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (cost accounting only; verdicts unchanged).
3701	        from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc_cost  # noqa: PLC0415
3702	        _run_id = _orc_cost._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get()
3703	        _cost_before = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id)
3704	
3705	        _parent_ctx = _ctxvars.copy_context()
3706	
3707	        def _verify_in_context(_s: str) -> SentenceVerification:
3708	            return _parent_ctx.copy().run(
3709	                _verify_one_findings_sentence,
3710	                _s, evidence_pool,
3711	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3712	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3713	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3714	            )
3715	
3716	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_verify_workers) as _pool:
3717	            _findings_results = list(_pool.map(_verify_in_context, _findings_to_verify))
3718	
3719	        # Reconcile the parallel verify spend into the parent budget gate (see note above).
3720	        _cost_delta = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id) - _cost_before
3721	        if _cost_delta > 0:
3722	            _orc_cost._add_run_cost(_cost_delta)
3723	            _orc_cost.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the pool breached the cap
3724	
3725	    for v in _findings_results:
3726	        # Keep iff verified (a re-anchored rescue is returned already is_verified=
3727	        # True), drop otherwise — identical decision to the legacy inline branch.
3728	        if v.is_verified:
3729	            kept.append(v)
3730	        else:
3731	            dropped.append(v)
3732	
3733	    # Limitations: telemetry-grounded verification if block supplied,
3734	    # else pass-through (M-204 backward-compat).
3735	    limitations_sentences = split_into_sentences(limitations_text)
3736	    for s in limitations_sentences:
3737	        # I-pipe-015 (#1240): same malformed-token canonicalization as findings.
3738	        if _honest_tokens:
3739	            s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)

3523	
3524	    # I-deepfix-001 M6: ADDITIVE cross-source analytical pass. DEFAULT-OFF => byte-identical (no import,
3525	    # no call). ON => append analytical sentences (two engine-licensed verified atoms) on top of the
3526	    # keep-all single-source units. Each analytical unit carries TWO distinct [#ev] tokens whose two-span
3527	    # footprint is a SUPERSET of either atom's, so the idx8 footprint-dedup above (applied identically
3528	    # below) can never collapse it against an atom — keep-all holds. The downstream
3529	    # _rewrite_draft_with_spans + UNCHANGED strict_verify tail gates each analytical sentence per clause.
3530	    if _cross_source_synthesis_enabled():
3531	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.cross_source_synthesis import (  # noqa: PLC0415
3532	            build_basket_agreement_map,
3533	            compose_cross_source_analytical_units,
3534	            cross_source_thread_consolidation_enabled,
3535	        )
3536	        # I-deepfix-001 FIX 3 (#1344): thread the consolidation AGREEMENT MAP so the cross-source
3537	        # analytical pass sees ``input_threaded=True`` and cross-basket CORROBORATION admits a plan-driven
3538	        # candidate (before FIX 3 the caller threaded only ``edges``, so the composer logged
3539	        # ``input_threaded=False degraded=True`` and agreement never fired from consolidation). DEFAULT-OFF
3540	        # (``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_THREAD_CONSOLIDATION``) => ``build_basket_agreement_map`` returns {} and this
3541	        # is byte-identical to threading the caller's original ``agree_map`` (None today). Built ONLY when
3542	        # the caller did not already thread one. Faithfulness-neutral: the map admits candidacy + telemetry;
3543	        # each emitted connective is STILL independently re-gated per built clause inside ``_process_pair``.
3544	        _threaded_agree_map = agree_map
3545	        if _threaded_agree_map is None and cross_source_thread_consolidation_enabled():
3546	            _threaded_agree_map = build_basket_agreement_map(section_baskets)
3547	        analytical = compose_cross_source_analytical_units(
3548	            section_baskets, evidence_pool,
3549	            writer_fn=writer_fn, verify_fn=verify_fn,
3550	            edges=edges, equiv_clusters=equiv_clusters, agree_map=_threaded_agree_map,
3551	            # Wave-2a: None unless the caller threaded the numeric merge-key lookup (only when
3552	            # PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR is on) => the comparator is never consulted otherwise (byte-identical).
3553	            numeric_key_by_cluster=numeric_key_by_cluster,
3554	        )
3555	        for unit in analytical:
3556	            if not unit or not unit.strip():
3557	                continue
3558	            spans = _resolved_spans(unit)
3559	            norm = _dedup_norm(unit)  # Fix 3a: citation-INSENSITIVE key
3560	            footprint = frozenset(spans)
3561	            # Same footprint-equality + text-identity dedup contract as the per-basket loop above (so a
3562	            # true-duplicate analytical unit collapses) — but the two-span footprint guarantees a real
3563	            # cross-source unit is never a subset of a single-source sibling.
3564	            if footprint and footprint in seen_numbers_by_footprint:
3565	                unit_numbers = _number_tokens(unit)
3566	                if not (unit_numbers - seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint]):
3567	                    continue
3568	                seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] = seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] | unit_numbers
3569	            # Fix 3b: text-identity collapse regardless of span backing; union citations onto survivor.
3570	            if norm and norm in seen_texts:
3571	                _si = text_to_out_idx.get(norm)
3572	                if _si is not None:
3573	                    out[_si] = _attach_citation_tokens(
3574	                        out[_si], [f"[#ev:{_e}:{_s}-{_en}]" for (_e, _s, _en) in spans]
3575	                    )
3576	                seen_spans |= spans
3577	                continue
3578	            if footprint and footprint not in seen_numbers_by_footprint:
3579	                seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] = _number_tokens(unit)
3580	            seen_spans |= spans
3581	            seen_texts.add(norm)
3582	            text_to_out_idx[norm] = len(out)
3583	            out.append(unit)
3584	    # P0-4 SEMANTIC DEDUP AT EMIT (2026-07-10): consolidate cross-basket same-MEANING units (the rotated-
3585	    # citation whole-block duplication) to ONE, unioning every collapsed instance's citations so NO source
3586	    # is dropped (§-1.3). Runs LAST so it sees every per-basket + companion + analytical unit. A distinct
3587	    # claim (different number/entity/population) has a different signature and survives.
3588	    out = _consolidate_same_meaning_units(out)
3589	    return out
